title: Enforce JSON output via XML delimiters plus a reformat-retry pass
description: Structured output relies on a ```json markdown fence (delegate.py:1506-1526) parsed by regex with a silent 'needs_review' fallback (1609-1624). Markdown fences are the least reliable delimiter — agents often emit prose around them or omit the language tag. Switching to explicit, unique XML tags (<result_json>...</result_json>) is far easier to extract unambiguously, and adding a single lightweight reformat-retry (send only the unparseable tail back to Haiku asking it to re-emit valid JSON in the tags) recovers most malformed responses instead of discarding them to needs_review.
proposed_changes: Change the output-format spec in build_prompt() to require the result inside <result_json>...</result_json> tags. In schemas.py extend extract_json_block() to try the XML-tagged form first, then fall back to the existing fenced/bare-JSON regexes for backward compatibility. In parse_agent_output() (delegate.py:1554), before returning the safe default, add one reformat-retry: invoke a cheap Haiku call with just the raw tail and the target schema, asking only for valid <result_json>. Cap at a single retry and audit-log when it fires.
